title: Nerv Logo is everywhere except
wikitext:
[]: Nerv Logo is everywhere, except...             Quote Post by Sodra » Sat Jun 29, 2013 5:05 pm Everyone knows the NERV logo But don't you think they go a little overboard with it? I mean, its everywhere. It's on the hangar door. It's on their little train carts. Their VTOL's The giant photon sniper they used (Even though that is owned by the JSSDF, they still put it on the little adapters they made for the Eva) Hell, its even on the rescue box they use for medical attention. Speaking of medical attention, apparently Rei uses Nerv distributed pills. Want to take a nice hot shower/sauna? Heres the logo of the place you work to calm down. I don't even know what these are (Forgive me, I haven't been in a public shower/sauna before.) The NERV logo is everywhere Except the one thing they are known for, The Evangelion. Nope. Nothing. No logo of any sort. So the question is, Why is there no NERV logo on the Eva?

[]:             Quote Post by Catamari » Sat Jun 29, 2013 5:22 pm Plausible Deniability. Maybe they ran out of money.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Sat Jun 29, 2013 5:36 pm Doesn't NERV make Evangelion for the purposes of SEELE and their version of Third Impact? Wouldn't that technically make them SEELE's Evangelion, with NERV merely used as some sort of manufacturing company? Kinda like how ILM or Stan Winston Studios don't own the rights to anything they design creatures for, they're just commissioned to design stuff that other studios already own. Heck, ILM doesn't even own the rights to the Star Wars designs despite the company being created specifically to create the special effects for the 1977 Star Wars film. Same thing goes here. NERV is merely "commissioned" to create SEELE's Evangelion, but they don't own the Evangelion despite being created specifically to be SEELE's Eva manufacturing company. Think about it. Where do you think NERV's budget comes from? They don't make an income by creating any goods or services to the public markets, do they? And I don't think it's even mentioned that taxpayer money goes the Eva programs either; just the JSDF programs that launch worthless missiles at the Angels before the Evas are called into action. So SEELE must be paying for everything NERV makes, therefore they must own everything NERV makes. I mean, there are other NERV bases all around the world that SEELE deals with. I'm pretty sure those other NERV centers don't own the Evas they make either.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sat Jun 29, 2013 5:38 pm Oh it's there, you just don't get many eva taint shots.

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Sat Jun 29, 2013 5:38 pm It requires too much production effort, so it's calculated laziness. Every case you posted where there is a logo is either a static piece or a 3DCG model. Adding logos to dynamic 2D animation is too much trouble. Having logos on the 3DCG Eva models but not on the 2D animation is inconsistent so might as well leave it out of both. The [s]logo[/s] Nerv label is actually there in promotional artwork. Note the text on the arm: NERV TOKYO-3 Eva-01  SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Atropos » Sat Jun 29, 2013 6:26 pm You don't suppose this could be a subtle reference to the Eva brand name being put on absolutely everything, do you? Most of those shots are from Rebuild... (Even as far as the original series goes, Anno is an otaku. He knew back then that his show would be merchandised to hell and back if it got popular.)

[]:             Quote Post by melonjaywalk » Sat Jun 29, 2013 9:29 pm Interesting point. The existence of the logo on everything simply for brand awareness that lends itself to sales outside the show. Why else would an organization dealing with pretty secret stuff, dedicated to humanity's future for better or worse, brand itself so heavily on mugs and buckets of all things. I suppose they'd wanna' feign that they're just another company and that's a good way of doing it. Or something.

[]:             Quote Post by Aiko Heiwa » Sat Jun 29, 2013 10:27 pm the nerv logo is actually on them, it's just really tiny and hidden and is right next to the words "Made in China" no i did not just make that up

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Sat Jun 29, 2013 10:42 pm Aiko Heiwa wrote: the nerv logo is actually on them, it's just really tiny and hidden and is right next to the words "Made in China" no i did not just make that up

[]:             Quote Post by nervshatter » Sat Jun 29, 2013 10:54 pm Nerv t-shirts and breakfast cereal.

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Sat Jun 29, 2013 11:48 pm Digging through NGE official artwork so far found 3 instances. Yamashita slapped the leaf logo on the horn once SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Sun Jun 30, 2013 6:04 am TMBounty_Hunter wrote: Yoh Yoshinari once put Nerv in text form on the forward part of the shoulder pylon SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Jun 30, 2013 6:13 am ^ Secondary tier at most.

[]:             Quote Post by Sodra » Mon Jul 01, 2013 4:11 am I was thinking that they didn't put a NERV logo on it because they didn't want to say they created god, just as you always mask the face of Eva. Also, I found an instance where they put the logo on. On the back of the Eva simulator. I'm sure this doesn't count though. Is the simulator considered an Eva? It seems like it was grown just like the others.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Mon Jul 01, 2013 6:33 am Sodra wrote: Is the simulator considered an Eva? It seems like it was grown just like the others.

[]:             Quote Post by Jurrasic » Mon Jul 01, 2013 7:50 am FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: I've no idea. It seems really dissimilar from the one in NGE, which looked like it was just Unit 01 plugged into some wires and stuff. (Great. Now I want a short film before FINAL that follows the day-to-day inner thoughts of the Simulator Unit.)

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Mon Jul 01, 2013 4:05 pm The logo probably isn't slapped on there because somebody may have figured that it wouldn't look nice. It wouldn't match Yui-sama's impeccable style.

[]:             Quote Post by Squigsquasher » Mon Jul 01, 2013 4:32 pm Something's just occurred to me about the simulation units. An Evangelion needs a soul in order for the pilot to be able to synchronize, right? That counts for the simulators as well, seeing as they're still Evas. The simulation units have human souls in them, locked in immobile, mutilated bodies, trapped forever. That is one of the worst possible fates I can imagine.

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Mon Jul 01, 2013 4:59 pm Not necessarily. First off we have no evidence to claim souls are in any Evas in NME other than Yui in Eva-01. The Eva simulator in 1.0 has neither a core nor an proper entry plug (It's labled a simulation plug). SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Squigsquasher » Mon Jul 01, 2013 5:01 pm True. However, in the case of the original series, where it is confirmed that Evas need souls to work, and the simulators are definitely (albeit mutilated) Evangelions, there is a possibility. *shudder*
